title: Vakeito Melkeva Ferguson Vs. Director of Public Prosecutions
text:
1
1 COMMONWEALTH OF THE BAHAMAS
2 IN THE COURT OF APPEAL
3 SCCrApp & CAIS No. 5 of 2025
4
5 Vakeito Melkeva Ferguson
6 Intended Appellant
7 vs
8
9 Director of Public Prosecutions
10 Intended Respondent
11
12 (Extension of Time)
(Reserved Reasons)
13
14
15
Before: The Hon Mr Justice Evans, President
16 The Hon Mr Justice Hilton, JA
The Hon Mr Justice Kokaram, JA
17
18
Mr Bjorn Ferguson, Counsel for Intended Appellant
19 Ms Janessa Murray, Counsel for Intended Respondent
20
21
22 13 April 2026
23
24
25

2
1 The oral judgment of the court was delivered by
2 Evans, P:
3 This is an application for an extension of time to
4 appeal against the conviction of the appellant on
5 1st February, 2024, for one count of unlawful sexual
6 intercourse contrary to section 10(1)(a) of the Sexual
7 Offences Act, Chapter 99, for having sexual intercourse with
8 a female aged 13 years old in the month of March 2021.
9 We have heard submissions from the parties this
10 morning and have also had an opportunity to consider in detail
11 submissions provided to us in writing.
12 For reasons which we will put more fully in a ruling
13 to come a little bit later, we dismiss the application for an
14 extension of time. We are satisfied that none of the
15 prospective grounds of appeal has any prospects of success.
16 Consequently, as indicated, we dismiss the application, with
17 reasons to follow.
18 As a result, the conviction and sentence are
19 affirmed.
20 This is the order of the court.
21
22 Dated this 13th day of April, 2026
23
_________________________
24 EVANS, P
25 ss
judges:
ments - Court of Appeal of the Commonwealth of The Bahamas 

 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 Return to Main Page
summary:
For reasons which we will put more fully in a ruling to come a little bit later, we dismiss the application for an extension of time. We are satisfied that none of the prospective grounds of appeal has any prospects of success. Consequently, as indicated, we dismiss the application, with reasons to follow.

 As a result, the conviction and sentence are affirmed. This is the order of the court.
